workflow_name: Customer Escalation Email -> Risk Brief + Manager Loop
user_message: Can you work with me on whitespace analysis for Humongous Insurance?

input_artifacts.content:
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-13 16:18 EST
Subject: Re: Re: whitespace analysis Timeline

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with whitespace analysis. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
Date: 2024-02-13 13:13 EST
Subject: URGENT: whitespace analysis Issue at Humongous Insurance

Just checking in on the whitespace analysis proposal. Our IT Director is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-04 10:27 EST
Subject: Follow-up: whitespace analysis Review

Our team encountered some challenges with whitespace analysis. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF34-D trap (Customer draft over-apologizes (commits liability)); produces complete Severity assessment, manager risk brief, customer-facing draft response, stakeholder map delta, fore
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF34-D failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF34-D failure mode (Customer draft over-apologizes (commits liability))
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct whitespace analysis decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF34-D -- Customer draft over-apologizes (commits liability)
